What to do

This course, MAT223 Te Ara Pāngarau, is designed to be flexible. You will choose your own learning methods and standards, and work at your own pace. You will also sit assessments when you feel ready. Your teacher will act as a mentor, helping you plan your work, with difficult questions, and providing feedback.

Key requirements

  • You need to pass at least four achievement standards this year.
  • Ensure you meet the prerequisites for the courses you want to take in Year 13.
  • If you haven't met the 'numeracy' requirement of NCEA, talk to your teacher.
  • Year 12 students should focus on Level 2 standards, but two Level 3 standards are also available.
  • You can only resubmit an internal assessment to change a 'Not Achieved' grade to 'Achieved'.

Tips

  • You are in control of your learning and can work at your own pace.
  • Teachers will act as mentors, guiding you through the course.
  • It's important to discuss with your teacher if you want to do a standard at a higher level, as it might make getting endorsements harder.
  • Remember to bring your laptop, headphones, pens, pencil, and calculator to every lesson.

Dates & deadlines

  • The first internal assessment AS2.3 cannot be sat within the first 3 weeks.
  • The latest date to sit the first internal assessment AS2.3 is the end of Week 7.
  • In Week 4, you will receive more information about sitting internal assessments.
  • All external topics must be finished before the derived grade exams in Week 8 of Term 3.
  • If you want to do two external standards, you must do them immediately after finishing 2.3.
  • There will be time after the derived grade exams to complete other internal topics.
  • End-of-topic tests need to be sat within one lesson.
